item.title: CATL Overtakes PetroChina in Market Value as Green Bets Ramp Up
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. has once again overtaken oil giant PetroChina Co. in market value, as investors pile into the battery maker on war-driven optimism over green energy.

item.title: China and India Signal a Decade of Cautious Climate Action
item.description: Are two of the world’s biggest polluters doing enough to help the world avoid catastrophic climate change? Lili Pike and Akshat Rathi discuss.

item.title: US to Preserve Billions in Hydrogen Funds Pegged for Termination
item.description: The Trump administration plans to preserve billions of dollars in funding for hydrogen ventures and other projects previously earmarked for termination.

item.title: UK Car Industry Seeks Changes to EU’s ‘Made in Europe’ Agenda
item.description: The European Union should urgently adjust a proposed law that will discriminate against British automobile manufacturers and undermine €80 billion ($94 billion) of annual trade, according to a trade group.

item.title: Bessent ‘Optimistic’ Gas Will Drop to $3 Per Gallon Over Summer
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said he was “optimistic” that the price of gasoline will abate over the crucial summer driving season, retreating back to $3 a gallon.

item.title: European Clean Tech Investors Make the Case for Geothermal
item.description: The energy source is emissions-free and can’t easily be attacked by adversaries.

item.title: Freedom Forever Files for Bankruptcy as Solar Industry Woes Deepen
item.description: Freedom Forever, a once thriving residential solar installer, joins a growing list of bankruptcies as distress in the US clean energy sector deepens under an unfriendly Trump administration and hopes for buyouts fade.

item.title: EU Favors Central Buying of Imported Carbon Credits Next Decade
item.description: The European Union said it prefers controlled purchases of international carbon credits over the acquisition of them by individual companies, as it wants to ensure only high-quality projects count toward its ambitious climate target.

item.title: Defense Firms Are Asking EIB for Help Navigating Energy Crisis
item.description: Defense firms are increasingly turning to the European Investment Bank for help to shift to alternative energy sources, as the Iran war drives up the price of oil.

item.title: Amundi Plans to Hire for Blended Finance in Push for More Deals
item.description: Amundi SA is planning to add headcount to target deals that combine private and public capital for emerging-markets projects.

item.title: England Has 1.2 Million Buildings at Risk of Flooding With No Defenses
item.description: A new study has found that 1.2 million buildings at risk of flooding in England currently aren’t included in any of the country’s flood defenses.

item.title: Turkey Demands Decisive Climate Action Ahead of New Global Talks
item.description: Turkey urged nations to commit to new action to tackle climate change as it confirmed details of talks scheduled in the Pacific region ahead of a formal United Nations summit.

item.title: China’s Control Over Tech Is Threatening India’s Manufacturing Dreams
item.description: India’s biggest companies are struggling to break Beijing’s grip on production of batteries, EVs and critical minerals.

item.title: Caterpillar Acquires Self-Driving Electric Tractor Startup
item.description: Monarch is one of a growing number of green companies struggling to commercialize their technology.

item.title: New York to Select Site for New Nuclear Power Plant This Year
item.description: New York state expects to select a community this year to host at least 1 gigawatt of new nuclear energy, signaling a push to accelerate reactor development as electricity demand climbs.

item.title: NYC, Washington Brace for Record Heat, Power Demand Set to Rise
item.description: Early-season heat is expected to sweep across New York City and large parts of the US, threatening hundreds of records and driving up energy demand.

item.title: Delta Waters Down Net-Zero Target to an ‘Aspiration’
item.description: The airline also erased its green fuel pledge.

item.title: Bargain Car Hunters Drive Next Wave of US EV Sales
item.description: Used electric cars and lower-priced models see big sales gains as new EVs stall.

item.title: Microsoft Rejects Speculation It’s Axing Carbon Removal Business
item.description: Microsoft Corp., the world’s biggest investor in carbon removal projects, rejected speculation that it is abandoning its efforts to build out the technology.

item.title: Delta Air Lines Walks Back Sustainable Fuel, Net-Zero Goals
item.description: The carrier erased a pledge to use 10% sustainable aviation fuel by 2030 and recast its net-zero goal as an “aspiration.”

item.title: Stegra Gets €1.4 Billion From Wallenberg-Led Group to Save Plant
item.description: Ailing Swedish startup Stegra AB has secured funding from the influential Wallenberg family to help the company complete what will be the world’s biggest green-steel plant.

item.title: China’s Battery Exports Surge as War Drives Energy-Supply Crunch
item.description: China’s lithium battery exports surged in the first quarter, reinforcing early signs of demand for alternative power sources to counter the global energy-supply crunch arising from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Shark Nets Spark Furor at Club Med’s First South African Resort
item.description: Club Med SAS has landed in the middle of a furor over the best way to keep sharks from attacking tourists at its more than $120 million South African resort.

item.title: Energy, Chemical Sectors Urge EU to Ease Imminent Emission Rules
item.description: A coalition of energy companies, chemical makers and trade associations are asking the European Union to urgently consider changes to the bloc’s upcoming regulations on methane emissions.

item.title: KoBold Says Congo Lithium Exploration Campaign ‘World’s Biggest’
item.description: KoBold Metals Co. has begun exploring for lithium in the Democratic Republic of Congo, in what the US firm described as the largest-ever campaign for the battery metal.

item.title: Microsoft Staff Tell Some Carbon Capture Companies It’s Pausing Deals
item.description: It’s the world’s biggest carbon removal program.

item.title: Booming Weather Prediction Markets Could Improve Forecasting
item.description: Increasingly popular prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket may improve forecasting by aggregating knowledge

item.title: India Predicts Below-Normal Monsoon, Threatening Crop Production
item.description: India is likely to witness a below-average monsoon this year, adding pressure on farmers who are already facing higher input costs following the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Fund Managers Seek Reporting Crackdown After Philippines Scandal
item.description: Money managers led by BNP Paribas Asset Management and Robeco Institutional Asset Management BV are putting pressure on the Philippine government to introduce stricter reporting standards for state-backed projects, following a corruption scandal tied to flood infrastructure.

item.title: EU to Recommend Lower Energy Taxes to Offset Surging Oil and Gas
item.description: The European Commission is set to recommend lowering energy taxes and power grid charges to boost the uptake of clean technologies while softening the blow from surging oil and gas prices.
